pattern: A frequency table over the fixed range 0..n-1 finds the two values that were counted twice in a single linear pass.
hints: The numbers all live in a known, small range (0 to n-1), which is a strong hint to count occurrences with a fixed-size array instead of a hash map.
Walk the array once, bumping a counter per value; the moment a counter reaches two, you've found one of the sneaky numbers.
Collect every value whose counter reaches exactly two -- there are always exactly two of them -- and return that pair.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, small n with the two duplicates adjacent
the two sneaky numbers are the smallest and largest possible values
duplicates are consecutive values, must not be confused with a run
one duplicated value is 0, easy to mishandle with falsy checks

stress.description: random n with two random distinct values duplicated
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(100, size))
    nums = list(range(n))
    rng.shuffle(nums)
    a, b = rng.sample(range(n), 2)
    nums.append(a)
    nums.append(b)
    rng.shuffle(nums)
    return [nums]
